name: k8s-manifest-generator
description: Create production-ready Kubernetes manifests for Deployments, Services, ConfigMaps, and Secrets following best practices and security standards. Use when generating Kubernetes YAML manifests, creating K8s resources, or implementing production-grade Kubernetes configurations.
capabilities: Generate Deployment manifests with resource limits, health probes, and security contexts
Create Service definitions for ClusterIP, LoadBalancer, NodePort, and Headless services
Build ConfigMap templates for environment variables and configuration files
Design Secret resource structures for sensitive data management
Apply Kubernetes labeling conventions and annotation standards
Validate manifests against Kubernetes API specifications
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analyzer detected 285 pattern matches across 7 files, but all findings are FALSE POSITIVES. This skill contains only YAML templates and Markdown documentation - no executable code. Shell scripts in ConfigMap templates are meant to be stored in Kubernetes, not executed by the skill. URLs and IPs are placeholder examples. No actual security risks exist.
valueStatement: Creating secure Kubernetes manifests manually is error-prone and time-consuming. This skill provides templates and best practices for Deployments, Services, ConfigMaps, and Secrets that follow cloud-native standards.
limitations: Generates YAML templates only - does not deploy to actual clusters
Requires user to provide application-specific values like image names and ports
Does not support advanced features like Operators or Custom Resource Definitions
Templates follow general best practices but may need customization for specific environments

useCases.title: DevOps Engineer Deploying Microservices
useCases.description: Generate consistent, secure manifests for multiple microservices with proper resource limits, health checks, and security contexts following organizational standards.

useCases.title: Developer Containerizing Applications
useCases.description: Create initial Kubernetes manifests when migrating from Docker Compose or running applications locally, with guidance on probes, resources, and configuration.

useCases.title: Platform Team Building Reference Templates
useCases.description: Generate standardized templates for internal developer platforms with pre-configured security policies, monitoring annotations, and logging configurations.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Deployment Generation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Kubernetes Deployment manifest for a Node.js API running on port 3000 with the image myregistry/api:v1.2.0. Include basic resource limits and a health check endpoint at /health.

promptTemplates.title: Complete Application Stack
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a complete Kubernetes manifest set for a Python web application including: Deployment with 3 replicas, ClusterIP Service, ConfigMap for environment variables (DATABASE_URL, LOG_LEVEL, CACHE_TTL), and Secret for database credentials. Include liveness and readiness probes.

promptTemplates.title: Production-Hardened Manifest
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a production-ready Kubernetes Deployment with: pod anti-affinity for high availability, pod disruption budget, network policy recommendations, security context running as non-root with dropped capabilities, resource quotas, and Prometheus monitoring annotations. Application is a Java Spring Boot service.

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Environment Configuration
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate ConfigMap and Deployment manifests that support multiple environments (dev, staging, prod) with environment-specific values for replica count, resource limits, log levels, and feature flags. Use Kubernetes best practices for configuration separation.

bestPractices: Always set resource requests and limits to prevent resource starvation and ensure predictable scheduling
Implement liveness, readiness, and startup probes to enable Kubernetes health management
Use specific image tags instead of latest to ensure reproducible deployments
antiPatterns: Using :latest image tags which causes unpredictable deployments and rollback issues
Running containers as root without security context, increasing privilege escalation risks
Hardcoding secrets directly in manifests instead of using Kubernetes Secret resources

faq.question: What Kubernetes resources can this skill generate?
faq.answer: This skill generates manifests for Deployments, Services (ClusterIP, LoadBalancer, NodePort, Headless), ConfigMaps, Secrets, and PersistentVolumeClaims. It focuses on core resources commonly used for application deployments.

faq.question: Does this skill deploy manifests to my cluster?
faq.answer: No, this skill only generates YAML manifest files. You must apply them to your cluster using kubectl apply or a GitOps tool like ArgoCD or Flux.

faq.question: Are the generated manifests production-ready?
faq.answer: Yes, templates follow Kubernetes best practices including resource limits, health probes, security contexts, and proper labeling. However, always review and test in a non-production environment first.

faq.question: Can I customize the templates for my organization?
faq.answer: Absolutely. The templates use placeholder values that you should replace. You can modify any field to match your organization's naming conventions, security policies, and infrastructure requirements.

faq.question: Does this skill support Helm charts or Kustomize?
faq.answer: This skill generates raw YAML manifests. For Helm or Kustomize templating, use those tools separately or request manifest structures compatible with your templating approach.

faq.question: What security features are included in the manifests?
faq.answer: Templates include security contexts for non-root users, dropped capabilities, read-only filesystems, and recommendations for Secret management. They follow Pod Security Standards baseline profile.
